behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure boundary is the module handoff where three source collectors feed the summary input payload, not a single isolated function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch this; in-process fakes for the three source clients are sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: a unit test of only a formatter could miss wiring bugs where one collector is never invoked or dropped before handoff. LLM-DEP: correctness here is about payload completeness before generation, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified because behavior text is only about 'content passed to the summary-generation step' (pre-delivery, no real infra requirement). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text does not require evaluating model decisions, only input inclusion.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real orchestration modules with fake source adapters (metrics, issue tracker, deployment log) returning distinct sentinel values; invoke report build and assert the exact payload sent to summary generation contains all three sentinels and source sections.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is in cross-module error propagation (source adapter failure -> report assembler fallback section) rather than pure local logic. REAL-INFRA: no real network or subprocess is required; a fake adapter that raises timeout/unavailable errors can reproduce the boundary. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only exception helpers could miss orchestration abort behavior. LLM-DEP: requirement is deterministic fallback behavior, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: no chained model decisions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no dependence on real AI output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-stage handoff requirement in behavior text. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on graceful degradation of one source, which is reproducible with in-process module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with two healthy fake sources and one fake source raising an unavailable error; assert execution completes, output contains a `data unavailable` marker for only that section, and other sections are present.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stubbing generation with fixed text cannot expose factual distortion/omission in real narrative generation. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on generation quality ('accurately represents key values'), which is model-judgment dependent and can vary across runs/prompts. Deterministic fixture assertions would hide real failures in what the model actually writes. MULTI-STEP: this is a single generation-quality check, not a multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because behavior text requires semantic accuracy of the narrative itself, which a deterministic stub cannot validate.
behaviors.test_description: Use representative real input datasets with known key values, call the real summary-generation dependency, and score outputs against required facts (must-include values, no contradictions, tolerances for wording). Run multiple trials to detect variance-related failures.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, which crosses real network/email provider boundaries and cannot be faithfully proven with in-process mocks. MOCK-HIDE: mocked mail transport can pass while real SMTP/API auth, routing, MIME formatting, or provider acceptance fails. LLM-DEP: behavior checks delivery success, not model decision quality; generation can be deterministic for this test. MULTI-STEP: not a multi-agentic sequence requirement. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly,' which requires real external delivery infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script end-to-end in a test environment using real reachable internal sources (or system-level test doubles exposed over HTTP) and a real test mailbox; assert inbox receipt, recipient correctness, and expected report body/attachments.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this failure boundary spans delivery failure handling plus persistence across runs of a scheduled script; real process/run boundary and filesystem persistence are central. MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks can miss real failure modes in file persistence, path/permission issues, and retry behavior across separate invocations. LLM-DEP: assertions are about fallback/retry mechanics, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: sequential runs are required, but not multiple model decisions. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because behavior text requires retry 'on the next scheduled run,' implying cross-run/system behavior that can fail despite in-process orchestration tests.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute the real script with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; assert report file is persisted locally with retry metadata. Run 2 (next scheduled invocation simulation as a separate process): restore email service and execute again; assert queued report is retried and delivered, and local fallback state is cleared/updated correctly.
